name: Penicillin G benzathine
executiveSummary: Treatment of mild and moderate infections caused by susceptible gram-positive microorganisms or for prophylaxis of infections caused by these microorganisms. Syphilis.
indications:
## Main indications

Treatment of mild and moderate infections caused by susceptible gram-positive microorganisms or for prophylaxis of infections caused by these microorganisms. Syphilis.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Powder vial with lyophilized powder containing 1.2 and 2.4 MIU (Galtamicina, Klonal, Fabra, Richet, Pen Di Ben).
adulto.reconstitution: 5 mL SWFI (1.2 MIU); 10 mL SWFI (2.4 MIU). Conc: 0.24-0.12 MIU/mL.
adulto.diluent: 1.2 MIU in 5 mL SWFI or 2.4 MIU in 10 mL SWFI.
adulto.administration: Deep IM: Yes. Shake gently to allow powder wetting, then shake vigorously until complete dissolution. Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: No.
adulto.notes: Must not under any circumstances be administered by IV route due to risk of fatal ischemia.

pediatrico.dose: Syphilis: 50,000 IU/kg/dose, 1 to 3 doses according to duration of disease; maximum dose: 2,400,000 IU/dose. Secondary rheumatic fever prophylaxis: ≤ 27 Kg: IM: 600,000 IU every 3 to 4 weeks; > 27 kg: IM: 1,200,000 IU every 3 to 4 weeks.
pediatrico.presentation: Powder vial: 1,200,000 IU - 2,400,000 IU (lyophilized or suspension)
pediatrico.notes: Use with caution in children under 2 years. Penicillin G benzathine is administered weekly.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- Use immediately. Discard remainder if any.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- Not applicable.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Allergic reactions, mainly cutaneous. Hemolytic anemia, leukopenia.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Penicillin BENZATHINE (code 0162, ATC J01CA).
Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.

Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
